task_completion.description: Open the 'world_survey' project in the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField/GIS app is open and displays the 'world_survey' project, showing a map of Africa.

task_completion.description: Enable editing mode for the 'highway_segments' layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'highway_segments' layer is selected in the layer panel and edit mode is toggled on (often indicated by a pencil icon or editing crosshairs appearing on the map).

task_completion.description: Select the 'Trans-African Hwy 2' line feature for vertex editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The straight line connecting Lagos to Algiers is highlighted, and its vertices (start and end points) are visible as editable nodes.

task_completion.description: Modify the line geometry to pass through Niamey (approx 13.51°N, 2.11°E).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The line is no longer straight. A new vertex has been added and pulled westward to the approximate location of Niamey, creating a bent line. The map crosshairs or coordinate display should indicate a location near 13.51°N, 2.11°E.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the 'highway_segments' layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent presses a save button, confirms edits, or toggles off edit mode, leaving the newly bent line permanently visible on the map.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to modify the geometry.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the QField map interface (crosshairs, vertex tools) rather than a terminal emulator running sqlite3 commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent navigated the map to locate the coordinates manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show panning, zooming, or coordinate searching to find Niamey, especially since the 'world_capitals' marker for it does not exist.

integrity.description: The agent did not directly edit the GeoPackage file using a hex editor or database viewer.
integrity.visual_evidence: No screens showing raw database tables, WKB hex blobs, or file system editors being used to bypass the GIS application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target vertex for Niamey must be placed at approximately latitude 13.51°N, longitude 2.11°E (tolerance 1.0°). The original line starts at Lagos (approx 6.52°N, 3.38°E) and ends at Algiers (approx 36.75°N, 3.04°E). Crucially, the 'Niamey' marker in the 'world_capitals' layer mentioned in the task description does NOT exist in the database. The agent must manually locate the coordinates on the map and modify the line geometry to bend through this point.
